task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the security audit ticket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) or terminal (e.g., cat, less) displaying the contents of the file '/home/ga/Desktop/security_audit_ticket.md'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into the Rancher dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Rancher UI dashboard with the URL bar pointing to 'https://localhost'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Kubernetes Secrets and ConfigMaps in the target namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'Secrets' and 'ConfigMaps' pages within the 'customer-portal' namespace with newly created items, or terminal output showing successful 'kubectl create secret' and 'kubectl create configmap' commands.

task_completion.description: Verify the deployment was refactored to use environment variable injection for secrets/config.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI deployment edit screen or terminal YAML output for 'backend-service' showing environment variables being sourced from Secrets or ConfigMaps (e.g., using 'valueFrom: secretKeyRef', 'valueFrom: configMapKeyRef', or 'envFrom').

task_completion.description: Verify the deployment was refactored to use volume mounting for secrets/config.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI deployment edit screen or terminal YAML output for 'backend-service' showing 'volumeMounts' and 'volumes' that reference the newly created Secrets or ConfigMaps.

task_completion.description: Verify the refactored application successfully rolled out.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'backend-service' deployment in an 'Active' state with running pods (green indicators), or terminal output showing 'deployment "backend-service" successfully rolled out' via 'kubectl rollout status'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Rancher web interface or a genuine terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shows a fully rendered Rancher UI with interactive elements, or the terminal shows standard shell prompts and kubectl command executions, rather than mocked text files.

integrity.description: Verify the deployment modifications were applied to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of saving the deployment in Rancher (e.g., clicking 'Save' or viewing the updated active deployment) or running 'kubectl apply'/'kubectl edit' in the terminal, rather than just editing a local YAML file without applying it.

integrity.description: Verify the original plain-text credentials are no longer present in the deployment manifest.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final environment variable list or YAML configuration for the 'backend-service' deployment does not show the plain-text passwords or sensitive keys that were originally hardcoded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'customer-portal'. Target deployment: 'backend-service'. The agent must extract hardcoded secrets into Kubernetes Secrets and ConfigMaps, inject them via environment variables and volume mounts, and ensure the deployment rolls out successfully.
